examples.title: Vulnerable Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange (MITM-prone)
examples.description: Basic Diffie-Hellman without authentication, vulnerable to MITM attack
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the vulnerability: Oscar intercepts and replaces public values, establishing separate shared secrets with both Alice and Bob. Neither Alice nor Bob can detect this attack without authentication.

examples.title: Authenticated Diffie-Hellman (STS Protocol)
examples.description: Station-to-Station protocol with digital signatures preventing MITM
examples.explanation: The STS protocol prevents MITM by requiring both parties to sign the exchanged public values with their private keys. An attacker cannot forge these signatures without the private keys, so they cannot substitute their own public values.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log p) for exponentiation + O(n) for signatures

examples.title: Certificate Pinning Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates how certificate pinning prevents MITM even with valid certificates
examples.code:
PROCEDURE VerifyConnection(serverURL, serverCert)
 // Pinned certificate hash for this domain
 pinnedHash ← GET_PINNED_HASH(serverURL)
 
 // Get hash of presented certificate
 presentedHash ← HASH(serverCert)
 
 // Compare hashes
 IF pinnedHash = presentedHash THEN
 RETURN TRUE // Certificate is pinned and matches
 ELSE
 // Check if issuer is system-trusted AND cert is valid
 IF serverCert.IS_VALID AND serverCert.ISSUER_IS_TRUSTED THEN
 // Second chance: check public key hash
 pkpinnedHash ← GET_PUBKEY_PINNED_HASH(serverURL)
 pkpresentedHash ← HASH(serverCert.PUBLIC_KEY)
 
 IF pkpinnedHash = pkpresentedHash THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // MITM detected - certificate doesn't match pinning
 LOG_SECURITY_EVENT("Certificate pinning violation for " + serverURL)
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Certificate pinning adds an extra layer of security beyond normal PKI verification. Even if an attacker has a valid certificate signed by a trusted CA, the pinned certificate check will fail, preventing the MITM attack.
